name: Double Cash

verdict.text: Still the best flat-rate card: 2% on everything, uncapped

earn_rates.category: Hotels, car rentals & attractions, booked via Citi Travel
Everything else: 1% when you buy, 1% when you pay it off

earn_note: The 2% is really two separate 1% events: 1 ThankYou Point per dollar spent, plus 1 more point per dollar once that spend is paid off. Carrying a balance or only making minimum payments delays (or effectively forfeits, on written-off debt) the second 1%; this card assumes you pay in full.

points.note: As of a 2024 program change, Double Cash earns ThankYou Points rather than a separate 'cash back' currency, but on its own it's still capped at a flat 1¢/point for cash, gift cards, or Amazon, with no transfer partners. The nuance: this card's points can be pooled with a Strata Premier or Strata Elite account (link them in Citi's online banking) to become transferable at 1:1 to airline/hotel partners, turning otherwise-capped cash-back points into travel currency. On its own, Double Cash is a pure 2% cash-back card in every way that matters.

transfer_partners.highlight: No transfer partners on this account alone. Double Cash's ThankYou Points are cash-and-merchandise-only unless pooled with a linked Strata Premier or Strata Elite account, which unlocks the full 22-partner list at 1:1 for the combined balance.
transfer_partners.recent_changes: Double Cash moved from a distinct 'cash back' ledger to the ThankYou Points ledger in 2024, enabling the pooling/transfer trick above for the first time; previously its rewards couldn't become travel points at all.

insurance.detail: Not offered (removed in 2019 and never reinstated)
Doubles the manufacturer's warranty up to 24 months · capped at original purchase price, $1,000/claim

protection_note: Minimal by design: this is a cash-back card, not a travel card. Citi stripped purchase protection and rental coverage from Double Cash in 2019 and hasn't brought them back; the one thing it does have is a decent 24-month extended warranty, which most no-fee cards don't bother with.
rental_note: No rental car coverage at all; use a different card for rentals.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee applies
services.detail: 3% on purchases made outside the US; not built for international use.

services.name: Citi Entertainment
services.detail: Presale access to concert, sports, and event tickets.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns 2% on their spend (into your account)
Same limited protections as the primary card

additional_cards.note: Free to add: an easy way to consolidate household spend onto the flat 2% rate.

timeline.text: Double Cash's rewards were moved onto the ThankYou Points ledger instead of a separate cash-back currency. Day-to-day value didn't change (still 1¢/point cash-out), but it made this card poolable with a Strata Premier or Strata Elite account for 1:1 airline/hotel transfers, a capability it never had before.
Citi removed purchase protection and rental car coverage industry-wide from lower-tier cards, including Double Cash; a 24-month extended warranty benefit was added back later as a partial offset.
